mission.title: kubeflow: User None is not authorized to list ... for namespace: anonymous
mission.description: User None is not authorized to list ... for namespace: anonymous. This issue affects 10+ users.

steps.title: Identify kubeflow troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kubeflow deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=kubeflow
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: User None is not authorized to list ... for namespace: anonymous

steps.title: Check current Namespace configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubeflow resources:
```bash
kubectl get namespace -A
kubectl describe namespace <name> -n <namespace>
```
**What steps did you take and what happened:**
Installed kubeflow 1.0.
While trying to create notebook server I am getting errors like:
```
User None is not authorized to list kubeflow.org.v1beta1.not

steps.title: Apply the fix for User None is not authorized to list ... for namespace: anony
steps.description:
The logs from the jupyter-web-app:
```
"GET /api/namespaces/anonymous/notebooks HTTP/1.1" 200 -
2020-02-05 00:42:38,949 | kubeflow_jupyter.common.auth | WARNING | No user credentials were found! Make sure you have correctly set the USERID_HEADER in the Jupyter Web App's deployment.
```
The decorator
```yaml
User None is not authorized to list kubeflow.org.v1beta1.notebooks for namespace: anonymous
```

steps.title: Confirm User None is not authorized to list ... for namesp is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: The logs from the jupyter-web-app:
```
"GET /api/namespaces/anonymous/notebooks HTTP/1.1" 200 -
2020-02-05 00:42:38,949 | kubeflow_jupyter.common.auth | WARNING | No user credentials were found! Make sure you have correctly set the USERID_HEADER in the Jupyter Web App's deployment.
```
The decorator function for every action in the Jupyter Web App hits [this function](https://github.com/kubeflow/k. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

User None is not authorized to list .v1.persistentvolumeclaims for namespace: anonymous

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubeflow installed or the issue environment reproducible.
